title: Emergency preparedness and older adults - Canada.ca
content:
## Emergency preparedness and older adults
- Age-Friendly Communities
- Emergency preparedness and older adults
- Injury Prevention for Seniors
Older people are disproportionately affected by disasters. With the aging population and the increase in disasters in Canada and globally, it is crucial to understand the factors that can place older people at risk and to consider their potential needs and contributions in all stages of emergency management. As such, the Division of Aging and Seniors works with a wide network of stakeholders to develop resources and tools for older adults, first responders, health professionals and emergency managers. Our objectives are:
- to minimize harm and negative health impacts to older adults before, during and after disasters;
- to bring an older adults' perspective to the emergency management field; and
- to help older adults and their families be prepared.

## What information is available?
You may also visit the following links for more information about older adults and emergency preparedness:
- Your Emergency Preparedness Guide
- Emergency Preparedness for Older Adults – Canadian Red Cross
- Closing the Gaps: Advancing Emergency Preparedness, Response and Recovery for Older Adults (PDF) – Canadian Red Cross
